reviews.review_description: Cant play with my friend because it keeps saying connection lost when we’re right next to each other in the same house trying to use co location.
Is hand tracking possible?”

Fun game but unfortunately the game is very laggy online. It's ok for the host but for the guest it's unplayable. When you move your character it's ok there is no lag but when trying to move an object it's very laggy. Very annoying for a game that needs precision

reviews.review_title: Great simple game but needs some options
reviews.review_description:
My autistic son loves this game, he loves stacking things all the time..

The biggest problem is he really wants to stack same colored objects, depending on his day.

Could you implement a toggle to turn off the color reject? Or maybe just have a free play mode where a kid can just stack what ever they want as much as they want and then hit the red button to blow it all up.

Simple but would go a long way for my son

developer_response.body: Great to hear you are playing our game with your son! Now the problem is that if we would add this option it would invalidate most of the second and third challenges as they are puzzle based. That being said we could add something so that it would be possible in the sandbox mode.

reviews.review_title: Easy to learn, hard to master
reviews.review_description: A fun and simple idea done well with a clean presentation. I particularly liked how the game shows how high your friends have been able to stack certain levels. The lack of haptics and haptic feedback does make the actual stacking a bit harder, but on e you get used to that the game is quite fun!

All the items in this game look like poorly 3d printed items, no detail at all. The game feels empty, I mean, it has the bones to be good but nothing else. At the games Current state, I'd be cool paying $3 bucks, definitely not $15

developer_response.body: e are sorry you think its not worth the price, we worked hard to deliver a fun product, with plenty of content and features that people can enjoy. With that being said, we would like to know what is that the game is missing for you? What would you like to see added? Feel free to reach out to us using this email: support@cyberneticwalrus.com

reviews.review_description: Total blast playing with a friend in MR. Just be prepared for some buggy behavior but it's still great fun.

developer_response.body: Hey do you mind sharing with us what bugs you have encountered? Feel free to reach out to us through our Discord https://discord.gg/DYS2ZpvBfm or the following email address: Support@cyberneticwalrus.com

reviews.review_title: I bought it for quest pro & 3
reviews.review_description:
Update(18/12/2024):
Thank you for devs that listeing our request and their efforts about the updates!
Finally, I could play with my kids using quest pro even 2 without buying one more quest 3! Revised the stars to 5 today!^^

I bought it for playing with my children with two device, but it is unpredictable of not support AR in quest pro. 
Dear dev, 
Could you consider to add AR support for quest pro in the future?
Either the effects is good or bad, I just want this is functional in passthrough. Many thanks!

developer_response.body: Thanks for revising your review, much appreciated!

reviews.review_description:
Does it Stack is super hard unless you find enough patience, and magic balancing spot of the lamp shade, dinosaur bones, multicolored artwork, odd looking trees, all the while avoiding explosions of same color objects.. 

Jumping into a multiplayer session with a friend is an absolute joy!
First day, but loving it so far!

reviews.review_title: A Fun Dive into Chaotic Creativity
reviews.review_description: Does It Stack? is more than just a puzzle game; it’s a sandbox for your imagination, where logic takes a backseat, and fun takes the wheel. If you’re ready to challenge the laws of physics (and your own expectations), this VR/AR gem will leave you grinning from ear to ear.
Plays like a tech demo. We bought it to play multiplayer together in AR, but it does not currently support the Quest Pro. Our Quest 3 works fine in AR.

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you think it is a fun game. Sadly the way AR works on the Quest Pro is different from the Quest 3 and we use some features that are not available on the Quest Pro. However you should be able to play in multiplayer VR mode using both devices!

reviews.review_description: Local co-op with my wife is extra fun. It's a game night without any IRL cleanup afterwards. It would be nice to see objects have different weights though.

This game has awakened the buried little child inside me, so yeah, I'm a 30 year old kid now. So what 👶

# The game in a few words:
- Fun
- precise (only when it comes to achieve the desired height, not in the physical sense, it still a game and can't simulate reality 100%, but its still good)
- stressful 👺
- Suitable for all ages (and genders 😆).

If you're someone who snaps quickly "like me 👺" , you might want to break something after playing it 💀. Or you could break that habit and actually have the patience to play with zero damage 👌

# things to mention:

- I would love to try it out with other mates as co-op/multiplayer in the room. So if it had a referral system to invite other friends with a small discount to jump in, that would be great✌️

-I know it meant to be simple, but It would be nice to make the texture and the look of the objects more realistic than plain.  Adding that as a toggle option in the settings to have the choice in between sounds good👍

btw i believe the guy who says "cheese" when u grab it is James May (AKA Captain Slow)😆
Love this game! Soooo much fun in mixed reality!

reviews.review_title: Loving it. Only doing easy at moment.
reviews.review_description: Developers have done a great job of making the physics of this perfect. Will give me many hours of "pleasure". Just don't let 2 colours touch!

"Does It Stack? is an absolute gem! The perfect blend of creativity and physics-based gameplay, it challenges your brain while keeping things fun and engaging. Addictive mechanics, vibrant visuals, and plenty of humor make it a must-play for puzzle lovers.  It is very addictive trying to stack random items into a tower...

Great physics based stacking game!

Trick, frustrating… yet very rewarding!
Love how it shows you your friends best attempts at each stack, so you can try to go one better than them.

Physics are good, with the balancing and stacking of each item one on top of the other. Remember to keep matching colours apart…. BOOOOOOMM!!!!

Love the UI, how it shows bonus tasks for each level, which you can attempt to try get bronze, silver or gold for each one… or you can just stick to the main level.

Each level gives you a target height for you to achieve, before moving onto the next level. Completing each level also gives you an object to collect.

Great work by the devs, and a new and very interesting experience for all ages

reviews.review_title: IT STACKS ON THE TOP SHELF
reviews.review_description:
This is an early impression review.
I got this only this afternoon and oh boy, I am loving it, it’s a lot of fun.
Who knew stacking random items on top of things could be so addictive.
Don’t stack the same colours together though.
Mixed reality is the way forward but Quest 2 owners can play in VR mode too.

If you want to play something completely different and fun then this is right up your street.
The game is great but the only issue I had is sometimes the challenges are hard but then again that might defeat the purposes of a challenge 😅any ways great game!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! And yep, those challenges are tough by design—where’s the fun without a bit of brain sweat, right? But don’t worry, most levels have more than one way to crack 'em. If you’re really stuck, there's always the handy skip button (we won’t judge!). Plus, our Discord’s got a whole channel dedicated to sharing and debating solutions. You’re not alone in the puzzle struggle!
